title: Diameter of Binary Tree
problem_statement: Return the length of the longest path between any two nodes in a binary tree, measured in number of edges.

examples.explanation: The diameter path goes through nodes [4,2,1,3] or [5,2,1,3].

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 10^4]
intuition: At each node, the longest path through that node is the sum of left and right subtree heights. Track the maximum as you compute heights.

approaches.name: Post-order height computation
approaches.description: Use recursion to compute the height of left and right subtrees and update the maximum diameter at each node as left height plus right height.
approaches.why_not: Brute-force all pairs of nodes requires O(n^2) and is inefficient.
interview_commentary: The diameter at a node is left height plus right height.
Use height recursion to compute both height and diameter in one pass.
The maximum diameter may occur at any node, not necessarily the root.

interview_qa.a: Because the longest path through a node connects the deepest left and right descendants.
It accumulates the best diameter seen during the recursive height computations.

interview_qa.q: What is the return value of the helper?
interview_qa.a: The height of the subtree rooted at the current node.

interview_qa.q: Can the longest path avoid the root?
interview_qa.a: Yes, the algorithm checks every node as a potential path center.
